message:
test(fcp-e2e): align gcal compliance test with flattened capability ids

Continues the per-connector capability-rename sweep. Google Calendar's
manifest has collapsed the two-level `gcal.events.{read,write}` shape
down to the single-level `gcal.{read,write}` shape, matching the
pattern used by sibling connectors. This test is updated accordingly:

- `GoogleCalendarConnectorAdapter` operation map entry for
  `gcal.get_event` now reports `capability:
  CapabilityId::from_static("gcal.read")` instead of
  `"gcal.events.read"`.
- `gcal_default_deny_compliance_suite_passes` mints a token with
  `gcal.write` (was `gcal.events.write`), invokes `gcal.get_event`
  which requires `gcal.read`, and expects the denial. The doc comment
  is updated to match.
- `handshake_request` operations list adopts the new capability name.

Assertions are unchanged in shape; only the string literals move. No
production code touched.
